title: campanula sarmatica subsp. calcarea
intro: Campanula sarmatica subsp. calcarea, also known as the White Harebell, is a type of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Campanula sarmatica subsp. calcarea include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and reduce inflammation, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health conditions. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and it is often used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
